claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO comprises nine WPs in three functional layers (3 research pillar: WP2 M1-36, WP3 M1-30, WP4 M3-36; 3 demonstrator: WP5/6/7 M12-42; 3 horizontal: WP1 M1-48, WP8 M6-48, WP9 M1-48)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (work_packages[].wp_id and title for all 9 WPs; three-layer grouping consistent with WP titles); docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json (tasks[].start_month and end_month per WP — WP2 T2-01 M1 through T2-03 M36; WP3 T3-01 M1 through T3-03 M30; WP4 T4-01 M3 through T4-03 M36; WP5/6/7 T*-01 M12 through T*-03 M42; WP1 M1-48; WP8 T8-01 M6 through T8-03 M48; WP9 T9-03 M1 through T9-01 M48)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Dependency map contains 16 confirmed inter-WP data-input edges forming an acyclic graph; 9 of these are pillar-to-demonstrator edges (WP2/3/4 to WP5/6/7)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (dependency_map.edges — 16 entries counted: WP2/3/4→WP5, WP2/3/4→WP6, WP2/3/4→WP7, WP2/3/4→WP8, WP5/6/7→WP8, WP8→WP9; all edge_type='data_input'; cycle_detected=false; pillar-to-demo subset WP2/3/4→WP5/6/7 = 9 edges)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The representative critical path is WP2→WP5→WP8→WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (critical_path_nodes: [WP2, WP5, WP8, WP9]; critical_path_analysis.representative_path_selection_rationale confirms WP2 selected as source node; tied_paths lists all 9 equal-length paths; section correctly qualifies this as 'representative')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Eleven milestones MS1–MS11 with specific titles and due months: MS1 M3, MS2 M12, MS3 M12, MS4 M15, MS5 M12, MS6 M24, MS7 M30, MS8 M36, MS9 M42, MS10 M45, MS11 M48
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json (milestones[] — all 11 entries verified: MS1 due_month=3, MS2=12, MS3=12, MS4=15, MS5=12, MS6=24, MS7=30, MS8=36, MS9=42, MS10=45, MS11=48; titles match section summaries)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Risk register addresses 11 critical risks; RISK-01 neuro-symbolic integration latency (Medium/High) with modular fallback and M6 integration testing; RISK-04 clinical dataset access delays (Medium/High) with M1 ethics applications and MIMIC-IV/eICU fallback; RISK-07 LLM baseline drift (High/Medium) with model-agnostic interfaces and annual re-evaluation M12/M24/M36; RISK-10 integration complexity (Medium/High) with shared API contracts M1-M3 and 6-month integration checkpoints
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json (risk_register[] — RISK-01 through RISK-11 confirmed: RISK-01 likelihood=medium/impact=high mitigation includes fallback modes and M6 integration testing; RISK-04 likelihood=medium/impact=high mitigation includes M1 ethics applications and MIMIC-IV/eICU; RISK-07 likelihood=high/impact=medium mitigation includes model-agnostic interfaces and M12/M24/M36 re-evaluation; RISK-10 likelihood=medium/impact=high mitigation includes API contracts M1-M3 and 6-month integration checkpoints)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Ethics-sensitive workstreams covering AI system risks, health-data handling, and human-participant/clinical-context considerations are governed through WP1 ethics compliance oversight and WP5 clinical validation governance
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Source: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json (ethics_assessment.issues: ETHICS-01 Category 8 AI, ETHICS-02 Category 4 health data, ETHICS-03 Category 2 human participants; governance_matrix Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee composition: ATU and NIHS; management_roles MGR-011 ATU Ethics Compliance Coordinator grounded in WP1 T1-03; MGR-012 NIHS Healthcare Ethics Oversight Officer grounded in WP5). Derivation: ethics issues ETHICS-01/02/03 map to WP1 oversight (ATU, T1-03) and WP5 clinical governance (NIHS) via committee membership; the section's claim is a logical summary of the governance assignment, not an explicit single statement in any source.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three governance bodies: General Assembly (biannual, all 8 partners, ATU chair) for strategic decisions; Project Management Committee (quarterly, all partners, ATU chair) for operational monitoring; Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU and NIHS, as needed) for ethics and quality oversight
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json (governance_matrix[0] General Assembly meeting_frequency='Biannual', composition=all 8 partners; governance_matrix[1] PMC meeting_frequency='Quarterly', composition=all 8 partners; governance_matrix[2] Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee composition=[ATU, NIHS] meeting_frequency='As needed'; management_roles[role_id=MGR-001] assigned_to=ATU responsibilities include 'Chair of the General Assembly and Project Management Committee')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Partner capability profiles: ATU world-class neuro-symbolic AI/automated planning/LLM reasoning; CERIA formal coordination protocols/game-theoretic distributed AI; BIIS deep learning/RAG/memory-augmented networks; NIHS de-identified clinical datasets/clinical validation pathways/healthcare AI ethics oversight; ISIA operational pilot factory/digital twins/manufacturing AI; ELI logistics operations across 80+ countries/real-world logistics data; FIIT AI evaluation frameworks/EDIH network expertise; BAL LLM fine-tuning/production-grade agent deployment/open-source European LLMs
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json (partners[].description verified for all 8: ATU 'world-class research...neuro-symbolic AI, automated planning, and LLM-based reasoning'; CERIA 'formal coordination protocols, distributed AI, and game-theoretic approaches'; BIIS 'deep learning...memory-augmented neural networks. Pioneers in retrieval-augmented generation'; NIHS 'de-identified clinical datasets and established clinical validation pathways'; ISIA 'digital twins, smart factory systems, and AI-driven process optimisation'; ELI 'operating in over 80 countries...real-world logistics operations data'; FIIT 'AI evaluation frameworks, benchmarking methodologies...EDIHs'); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json (roles[].key_responsibilities — ELI: 'Exploitation pathway for logistics sector adoption'; BAL: 'AI-on-demand platform integration and open-source release management'; FIIT: 'Coordination with Testing and Experiment Facilities; EDIH network dissemination and technology transfer'; NIHS: 'Ethics oversight for healthcare AI application')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Pillar deliverables mature by M36, demonstrators complete validation by M42, benchmarking concludes at M48
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Source: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (deliverables[].due_month: pillar finals D2-02 M36, D3-02 M30, D4-02 M36; demonstrator finals D5-02 M42, D6-02 M42, D7-02 M42; benchmarking D8-03 M48). Derivation: 'mature by M36' groups pillar final deliverables whose due_months are all ≤M36 (D3-02 at M30, D2-02 and D4-02 at M36); 'by M42' groups demonstrator final deliverables all at M42; 'by M48' reflects D8-03 at M48. Claim accurately represents the grouped pattern but is not an explicit summary field in the source.
